title: Block Dialogue — Core Communication
description: You just arrived in Slovakia. You meet someone for the first time. Use everything you learned.

contact.name: Marek
contact.role: Someone you just met in Slovakia

context: You just arrived in Bratislava. You bump into Marek, a friendly Slovak, on the street. Start a conversation using what you know.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.prompt: Marek greets you formally. How do you respond?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Dobrý deň! — the right formal daytime greeting. Always mirror the greeting you receive.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Dobrý deň is for daytime. Dobré ráno is morning only, Dobrý večer is evening — and Dovidenia is goodbye, not hello.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks how you are. You are doing well. What do you say?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Dobre, ďakujem. A vy? — you answered and returned the question. That is exactly how Slovaks expect this exchange to go.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: You are doing well — so Dobre, ďakujem is right. Always return the question with A vy? when speaking formally.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks if you speak Slovak. You speak a little but not well. What do you say?

choices.translation: I speak a little. I don’t speak well.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Hovorím trochu. Nehovorím dobre. — honest and clear. Marek will immediately speak more simply for you.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Hovorím trochu is the most accurate answer — it keeps the conversation open and tells Marek to keep it simple.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.translation: Is this your first time here?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks if this is your first time in Slovakia. It is. What do you say?

choices.text: Áno, som tu prvýkrát. Som turista.
choices.translation: Yes, it’s my first time. I’m a tourist.
No, it’s their first time here.
Yes, it’s his first time here.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Som tu prvýkrát — Som means I am. Je means he/she is, Sú means they are. When talking about yourself, always use Som.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: You are talking about yourself — use Som, not Je or Sú. Som tu prvýkrát. Som turista.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks where you are staying. You are at a hotel in the city centre. What do you say?

choices.translation: I’m staying at a hotel in the centre.
I’ll stay here for a long time.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Bývam v hoteli v centre — Bývam means where you live or stay. Idem is for where you are going right now.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Kde bývate asks where you are staying — use Bývam. Idem means I am going, Prídem means I will come, Zostanem means I will stay.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.translation: What do you need in Bratislava?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks what you need. You want to eat and need a good restaurant. What do you say?

choices.translation: I want to eat. I need a good restaurant.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Chcem jesť. Potrebujem dobrú reštauráciu. — Chcem + verb for what you want to do, Potrebujem + noun for what you need.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Use Chcem jesť for what you want to do, and Potrebujem reštauráciu for what you need.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Výborne! Je tu skvelá reštaurácia — choďte hore ulicou, potom na druhom rohu doprava, je to vedľa lekárne.
exchanges.translation: Excellent! There is a great restaurant — go up the street, then right at the second corner, it is next to the pharmacy.
exchanges.prompt: Marek gives you directions but speaks too fast. What do you say?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Hovorte pomaly, prosím — your most important survival phrase. Every Slovak will slow down immediately when they hear it.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: When someone speaks too fast, Hovorte pomaly, prosím is always the right move. Polite and effective.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.prompt: Marek slowed down but you still did not catch all the directions. What do you ask?

choices.translation: Can you write that down, please?
I don’t understand. Do you speak English?

exchanges.correctFeedback: Môžete to napísať, prosím? — perfect. Written directions are much easier to follow than spoken ones in a new language.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: You still did not catch everything — so Môžete to napísať is better than pretending you understood.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Samozrejme! Tu máte. Potrebujem ísť teraz. Dovidenia!
exchanges.translation: Of course! Here you go. I need to go now. Goodbye!
exchanges.prompt: Marek hands you the written directions and says goodbye. How do you close warmly and formally?

choices.translation: Goodbye! Thank you for the help!

exchanges.correctFeedback: Dovidenia! ďakujem za pomoc! — warm, polite, and perfect. You closed your first Slovak conversation like a pro.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Dovidenia is the formal goodbye — Čau and Ahoj are only for friends. Always say ďakujem when someone helps you.

completionMessage: 🎉 Well done! You greeted Marek, said how you are, admitted you are still learning Slovak, confirmed it is your first time in Slovakia, said where you are staying, explained what you want to eat, asked him to slow down, and said a warm formal goodbye. That is a complete real first conversation in Slovak!

questions.correctAnswerEn: I speak a little. I don’t speak well.

questions.marekQuestionEn: Is this your first time here?
questions.correctAnswerSk: Áno, som tu prvýkrát. Som turista.
questions.correctAnswerEn: Yes, it’s my first time. I’m a tourist.
I’m staying at a hotel in the centre.

questions.marekQuestionEn: What do you need in Bratislava?
questions.correctAnswerEn: I want to eat. I need a good restaurant.

questions.marekQuestionSk: Výborne! Je tu skvelá reštaurácia — choďte hore ulicou, potom na druhom rohu doprava, je to vedľa lekárne.
questions.marekQuestionEn: Excellent! There is a great restaurant — go up the street, then right at the second corner, it is next to the pharmacy.

questions.correctAnswerEn: Can you write that down, please?

questions.marekQuestionSk: Samozrejme! Tu máte. Potrebujem ísť teraz. Dovidenia!
questions.marekQuestionEn: Of course! Here you go. I need to go now. Goodbye!
questions.correctAnswerEn: Goodbye! Thank you for the help!
